ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR NOV-04-2014 CONTINUED TO JAN-06-2015 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR FAILURE TO OBTAIN AN ANSWER(S) FROM, OR ENTER DEFAULT(S) AGAINST, DEFENDANT(S) MARIKA DUBIN RUSSELL MD. ** NOTE: REQUEST FOR DEFAULT REJECTED SEP-26-2014. ** THE NOV-04-2014 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT MARIKA RUSSELL'S MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY WITH REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTION AGAINST PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT THIS MOTION IS UNOPPOSED. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO COMPEL ANSWERS TO INTERROGATORIES AND RESPONSES AND PRODUCTION TO REQUESTS FOR DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF LACK OF A TIMELY RESPONSE TO THESE DISCOVERY REQUESTS WAS NOT JUSTIFIED. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFF HAS WAIVED ALL HIS OBJECTIONS. PLAINTIFF TO SERVE VERIFIED RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES NO LATER THAN MARCH 6, 2015. PLAINTIFF TO SERVE VERIFIED RESPONSES AND TO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS NO LATER THAN MARCH 6, 2015. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF TO SERVE A RESPONSIVE STATEMENT TO DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR STATEMENT OF DAMAGES PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 425.11(B) IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF TO SERVE HIS VERIFIED RESPONSE NO LATER THAN MARCH 6, 2015. DEFENDANTS REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF JOESIAH BELL TO PAY SANCTIONS IN THE AMOUNT OF $630.00 ($570 IN ATTORNEY FEES AND $60 IN FILING FEES ) NO LATER THAN MARCH 6, 2015. CHECK TO BE MADE PAYABLE TO DEFENDANT MARIKA DUBIN RUSSELL, M.D. AND HER ATTORNEYS OF RECORD HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM, KATHERINE GALLO, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT .
Off Calendar Nov-10-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant has met its initial burden of production by submitting the declaration of Lorrie Thomas, R.N., L.N.C., who opines that all health care providers employed by the City and County of San Francisco met the standard of care in their care and treatment of Plaintiff. "When a defendant moves for summary judgment and supports his motion with expert declarations that his conduct fell within the community standard of care, he is entitled to summary judgment unless the plaintiff comes forward with conflicting expert evidence." (Munro v. Regents of University of California (1989) 215 Cal.App.3d 977, 985.) Plaintiff has not objected to or even responded to Defendant's motion or expert testimony. Thus, Plaintiff has not raised a triable issue of material fact as to whether the health care providers employed by Defendant met the relevant standard of care. Therefore, Defendant is entitled to summary judgment. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Charles Crompton; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/CC)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Joesiah Bell
Off Calendar May-20-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Corinna Meissner of Hassard Bonnington LLP telephone number 415-288-9800 for Defendant Marika Russell.

No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. The Court adopted the tentative as follows: DEFENDANT MARIKA RUSSELL'S Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff Joesiah Bell is GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART as follows: (1) GRANTED as to Form Interrogatory Nos. 6.4(c), (d), 6.5(c), (e), 10.3, 11.1(e), (f); (2) GRANTED as to all Special Interrogatories; (3) GRANTED as to the Request for Statement of Damages; (4) GRANTED as to the form of response to the Request for Admissions; (5) DENIED as to the Request for Production of Documents; and (6) DENIED as to Form Interrogatory No. 17.1 as there is no evidence that this Form Interrogatory was served concurrently with the Request for Admissions. The Request for Sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $250. All verified responses to be served and sanctions to be paid within fifteen (15) days of notice of entry of order. Order signed and modified.

Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/JPT)

MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY/RESPONSE TO DISCOVERY
Off Calendar Feb-18-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMI TENCATI OF THE HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP, TEL. NO. 280-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MARIKA RUSSELL's MOTION TO COMPEL DISCOVERY WITH REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTION AGAINST PLAINTIFF is Granted. The court finds that this motion is unopposed. Defendant's motion to compel answers to interrogatories and responses and production to requests for documents is granted. The court finds that plaintiff lack of a timely response to these discovery requests was not justified. Therefore, plaintiff has waived all his objections. Plaintiff to serve verified responses to interrogatories no later than March 6, 2015. Plaintiff to serve verified responses and to produce documents no later than March 6, 2015. Defendants' motion to compel plaintiff to serve a responsive statement to defendant's request for Statement of Damages pursuant to C.C.P. Section 425.11(b) is granted. Plaintiff to serve his verified response no later than March 6, 2015. Defendants request for sanctions is granted. Plaintiff Joesiah Bell to pay sanctions in the amount of $630.00 ($570 in attorney fees and $60 in filing fees ) no later than March 6, 2015. Check to be made payable to defendant Marika Dubin Russell, M.D. and her attorneys of record Hassard Bonnington LLP.
Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem, Katherine Gallo, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/JPT
